lines.speaker: Priscilla
lines.text: Who — how did you get in here? The tunnels are sealed!

lines.speaker: Usagi
lines.text: I... I don't know where I am. There was a light, and then—

lines.speaker: Priscilla
lines.text: You are beneath Rome. In the catacombs of the faithful. And you are either a Christian or a spy.

lines.speaker: Usagi
lines.text: I am a Christian. My name is Usagi. I come from... far away.

lines.speaker: Priscilla
lines.text: Far away. Yes, I can see that. Those clothes are not Roman.

lines.speaker: Priscilla
lines.text: My name is Priscilla. I lead what remains of the community here. Nero's soldiers have been hunting us since the fire.

lines.speaker: Priscilla
lines.text: If you are truly a brother in Christ, then you are welcome. We need every hand we can get.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: You are the newcomer? Good. We need fighters, not more prayers.

lines.speaker: Usagi
lines.text: Fighters?

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: Nero burns us alive. He feeds our children to dogs. And what does the community do? Hide. Pray. Wait to die.

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: I was a legionary before I found Christ. I know how Rome fights. We can fight back.
What do you say?

choices.label: "You cannot fight Rome with swords alone."

lines.speaker: Marcellus
lines.text: We will see. When the soldiers come — and they will come — you will have to choose.

lines.speaker: Demas
lines.text: Ah, the traveler. Come, sit. I was just teaching.

lines.speaker: Demas
lines.text: The body is a prison, friend. A cage of flesh. What you do with it — eat, drink, even offer incense to the emperor — none of it touches your soul.

lines.speaker: Demas
lines.text: Only the spirit matters. And the spirit is already free.

lines.speaker: Usagi
lines.text: But Christ took on a body. He suffered. He died. That means the body matters.

lines.speaker: Demas
lines.text: A metaphor, surely. The divine spark descending into matter to teach us to escape it.
What do you say?

choices.label: "If the body does not matter, then the Incarnation was a lie."
"Your teaching gives people permission to betray."
"I need to think about this more."

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: You are the one Priscilla told me about. The rabbit from the East.

lines.speaker: Usagi
lines.text: You are... Peter? The apostle?

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: I am Simon. A fisherman. Everything else is what He made of me.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: Listen, little brother. I know what is coming. I have seen it in prayer.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: Nero will find us. Some will die. I will die. That is certain.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: But that is not failure. When the grain of wheat falls into the earth and dies, it bears much fruit.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: I denied Him once. Three times, in fact. I will not deny Him again.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: The question is not whether we will suffer. The question is whether our suffering will bear witness.

lines.speaker: Senator Petronius
lines.text: You must be the one they call Usagi. I have heard... interesting things about you.

lines.speaker: Senator Petronius
lines.text: I am Gaius Petronius. A senator. And I am, shall we say, sympathetic to your people's situation.

lines.speaker: Senator Petronius
lines.text: I can help. I have influence. Nero listens to me. I could arrange... protections.

lines.speaker: Senator Petronius
lines.text: All I ask is a small gesture of good faith. A public acknowledgment of Rome's gods. Not belief — just courtesy.
What do you say?

choices.label: "I will never burn incense to false gods."

lines.speaker: Senator Petronius
lines.text: How unfortunate. But the offer remains.

lines.speaker: Julia
lines.text: They will take us to the arena tomorrow. You know that.

lines.speaker: Usagi
lines.text: I will find a way to get us out.

lines.speaker: Julia
lines.text: No. Listen to me.

lines.speaker: Julia
lines.text: I have been praying for this moment my entire life. Not the dying — the witness.

lines.speaker: Julia
lines.text: Every person in that crowd will see a girl walk to the lions singing. And they will wonder: what does she have that makes her unafraid?

lines.speaker: Julia
lines.text: That question will outlast the Empire, Usagi. That question will convert Rome.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: You endured him. The Accuser. I can see his marks on you.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: He offered me the same bargain, once. In Gethsemane. I failed.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: But failure is not the end. The Lord restores. Three denials. Three affirmations. Do you love me? Feed my sheep.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: I am going now to where He needs me. They will crucify me upside-down, because I am not worthy to die as He did.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: But before I go — let me bless you, little brother.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: May the Lord who called you sustain you. May His light go before you across every century.

lines.speaker: Peter
lines.text: Go now. Egypt needs you. The desert is calling.
